body:
`ReferenceRepository` depends on `ObjectManager` interface:
https://github.com/doctrine/data-fixtures/blob/4892c74dd04a3ec7404b9586272d722325905c58/src/ReferenceRepository.php#L67

But futher in code `getUnitOfWork` method is called:
https://github.com/doctrine/data-fixtures/blob/4892c74dd04a3ec7404b9586272d722325905c58/src/ReferenceRepository.php#L127

`\Doctrine\Persistence\ObjectManager` interface doesn't contain this method. But `\Doctrine\ORM\EntityManagerInterface` contains.

Could you tell me, why there is a dependency on `ObjectManager` instead of `EntityManagerInterface`?

Thanks!
title: Why ObjectManager instead of EntityManagerInterface?

body:
## Use Case
The specific one on my mind: I disable youtube on my kids' devices, but quite a few web apps and learning management systems will have embedded youtube videos for a lesson. I would like to keep youtube blocked/disabled, but allow the embedded videos in an allow-list of websites/apps by selectively redirecting through a proxy such as invidious.

## Header Patterns
I have noticed in some requests with have an origin parameter but it is not consistent so it would be more robust to include matching on the referrer, such as :

```json
{
	"name": "Referer",
	"value": "https://www.youtube.com/embed/AQRagZY760w?rel=0&showinfo=0&playsinline=1&enablejsapi=1&origin=https://app.hoffmanacademy.com"
},
```

A pattern such as `header:referer:https://*.hoffmanacademy.com` might be slick and then users could match on cache, user agent, cookies, etc.

## window.location.href
This might be a more user friendly and "best" way to support my particular use case. Where the end user could add a list of sites in which the redirect rule would be enabled. 

## Solve at Proxy
Alternative to this FR, I could self-host invidious or peertube and block all video not on an allow list and selectively syncing youtube channel, solving it at the proxy  rather than at the browser.




I am more than happy to work on a PR but would really appreciate a plan/roadmap from someone familiar  to help me have a correct PR more efficiently.
title: [fr] source-based pattern matching

I primarily use a Git Bash shell in ConEmu for Git/Node/NPM related commands.  My platform is Windows 7 Ultimate x64.

After upgrading to build 151115 (and also with build 151119) I started experiencing various hangs and delays when trying to kill node/webpack processes via `ctrl-c`.  Sometimes it would seem to take 2 or 3 ctrl-c presses to kill the process; other times it would just hang and force me to kill ConEmu.

I also noticed that using `git commit` (which brings up an external editor for the commit message) does something odd after the editor is closed.  Rather than focus back to the bash commandline some odd context menu opens in the upper left corner of the screen when typing.

Now I don't know if there is some odd interaction with something else on my system, but I've experienced these symptoms for the past several days.  I downgraded to build 151109 and so far I haven't experienced any of these oddities.  I'm happy to provide some info, but I am working on a couple projects so it may be a little while before I can install different ConEmu versions again.
title: ctrl-c problems with build 151115 and above

body:
The `Lucene.Net.Search.FieldDoc.Fields` property returns `object[]`. However, according to [the documentation](https://lucenenet.apache.org/docs/4.8.0-beta00009/api/core/Lucene.Net.Search.FieldDoc.html), it is designed to be used with `string`, `int`, or `float`.

In Java, this was not an issue because there are numeric reference types that primitive numbers are wrapped in when passed around. But, since it would not be a good user experience to require wrapper classes for numeric types in .NET we should solve the boxing issue another way.
title: FieldDoc.Fields boxing issue

body:
In the xml document, the Signature Method and Digest Method are as follows:

`<ds:SignatureMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmldsig-more#rsa-sha256"/>`
`<ds:DigestMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#sha256"/>`

However, the Ruby-Saml gem parses the data as follows:

`:digest_method=>"http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#sha1", `
`:signature_method=>"http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#rsa-sha1"`

The Ruby-Saml parser's SHA response is incorrect. It should be sha256 instead of sha1. 

Do you know if there's a quick fix for this?

version 1.6
title: Signature Algorithm is Incorrect: Returning sha1 instead of sha256

body:
I just installed my first ever gem (my umask is 022) and ended up with:

```
$ ls -al /var/lib/gems/1.8/
total 28
drwxr-xr-x  7 root root 4096 2013-01-18 19:53 .
drwxr-xr-x  3 root root 4096 2013-01-18 19:49 ..
drwxr-x---  2 root root 4096 2013-01-18 20:06 bin
drwxr-x---  2 root root 4096 2013-01-18 19:53 cache
```

And was therefore unable to run the gem programs from bin because the dir is root owned and mode 750.

Generally when creating dirs in an installer-type software you want to specify the permissions you need explicitly rather than rely on umask.  The umask could always be 0 too and then we'd almost certainly get the wrong thing!

EDIT: Sorry, only repro'd on ubuntu 11.10, v1.3.7 so maybe fixed already.
title: don't obey umask when installing gems

Hi,

I tried to use the [columnNameForIndex: 4] method call on a FMResultSet instance. However, the column index 4 does not exist. FMDB crashes with the following Exception:

I would expect FMDB to handle this situation gracefully. You could check for a NULL C String before initializing the NSString ( FMResultSet.m line 400). I implemented a fix here: https://github.com/ccgus/fmdb/pull/430

Keep up the great work on FMDB.
Best regards,
Markus

body:
<!--

Welcome! - We kindly ask that you:

  1. Fill out the issue template below - not doing so needs a good reason.
  2. Use the forum if you have a question rather than a bug or feature request.

The forum is at: https://forum.xcsoar.org

Thanks for understanding, and for contributing to the project!

-->

I am trying to get XCSoar to run again natively on macOS. It used to work but now it does not anymore (for me). I managed to get it to compile (with these build fixes: https://github.com/sandrinr/XCSoar/tree/fix-macos) but when running it, it will just show a blank (black) window.


XCSoar versions having and not having the problem
-------------------------------------------------

Having: XCSoar master
Not having: don't know

Expected behavior
-----------------

XCSoar starts and shows fly/simulate screen.

Actual behavior
---------------

XCSoar shows blank black screen. Input seems to work, I can guess and press where the "Fly" and "Simulate" button is and press it. On the console I can see the program advancing but the window remains black.

Console output when invoking with `--fly` argument:
```
~/Projects/XCSoar (master)*$ output/OSX64/bin/xcsoar --fly
Free storage 66441953280 KB
Starting XCSoar XCSoar v7.0_preview15-macOS~git#e6d420eac1
Display dpi=230,230
OpenGL: npot=1 fbo=1 stencil=0x84f9
Initialise fonts
Startup dialog
Loading profiles
Loaded profile from /Users/raffaele/XCSoarData/default.prf
Loading language file
Loading input events file
OpenTerrain
Loading Topography File...
ReadWaypoints
Failed to read waypoint file: /Users/raffaele/XCSoarData/user.cup
Failed to open 'airfields.txt' from ZIP file
Start at terrain center
RASP load
No rasp data file: Failed to open ZIP archive /Users/raffaele/XCSoarData/xcsoar-rasp.dat
ReadAirspace
Failed to parse airspace file: airspace.txt
Failed to open 'airspace.txt' from ZIP file
Register serial devices
Opening device Built-in GPS & sensors
ProgramStarted
Free storage 66441952256 KB
cannot open /Users/raffaele/XCSoarData/lua/init.lua: No such file or directory
```

Steps to reproduce the behavior
-------------------------------

1. Compile on macOS using my branch with build fixes https://github.com/sandrinr/XCSoar/tree/fix-macos
1. Run run XCSoar: `output/OSX64/bin/xcsoar`


Do you have any idea what may have caused this?
-----------------------------------------------

My guess is that either SDL or OpenGL is not initialized/configured correctly for it to work properly on macOS. However, I don't really have SDL or OpenGL experience.

Do you have an idea how to solve the issue?
-------------------------------------------

Somebody with experience in XCSoar output backends might chime in and point me into a fruitful direction.
title: macOS: running XCSoar shows black window

body:
### Subject of the issue

using ruby git like this
```
require 'git'
puts Git.open(".").describe(nil, {long:true, tags:true, :dirty=>true, always:true})
```
output: `v0.0.0-2-g03626ae`

but running git directly produces the correct result 
```
git describe --long --tags --dirty --always
```
output: `v0.0.0-2-g03626ae-dirty`

### Your environment
* git version 2.24.0
*  ruby-git 1.5.0
* ruby 2.6.5p114 (2019-10-01 revision 67812) [x86_64-darwin19]

### Steps to reproduce
Generate a dirty repo, add a tag, add a commit, change a file,
now you have a dirty repo

run 
```
require 'git'
puts Git.open(".").describe(nil, {long:true, tags:true, :dirty=>true, always:true})
```


### Expected behaviour

there should be a _dirty_ in the output string (at the end)

### Actual behaviour

the dirty flag is not in the output
title: Git::Base(or Lib)#describe does not output dirty flag, even if it was requested

body:
The translations implementation in #420 expects the translation files to be specified relative to `RAILS_ROOT/surveys`. It should instead resolve them relative to wherever the survey itself is being read from.

If a survey is being read from a string (and it is not possible to specify a file for this relative determination), the current working directory should be used for resolving the translation files. (The benefit of this fallback over using `RAILS_ROOT/surveys` is that the calling code can control the working directory.)
title: Translation YAML should be loaded relative to source file, when possible
